[[File:Zamorakian spear detail.png|left|150px]]
The '''Zamorakian spear''' is a two-handed [[Weapons/Categories#Spear|spear]] obtained from the [[God Wars Dungeon]] as a drop from [[K'ril Tsutsaroth]] and his bodyguards, requiring an [[Attack]] level of 70 to wield. Unlike [[spear|other spears]], it cannot be [[poison]]ed, and has its highest bonus in [[stab]], while other spears have the same bonus on all melee attack styles. The Zamorakian spear offers the fourth highest stab bonus of all weapons at +85, shared with the [[Zamorakian hasta]], [[dragon hunter lance]], and [[crystal halberd]]. It is outmatched by the [[Ghrazi rapier]] with a stab bonus of +94, the [[Belle's folly|Belle’s folly]] with a stab bonus of +100 and the [[Osmumten's fang|Osmumten's Fang]] with a stab bonus of +105.

The Zamorakian spear is currently the strongest spear in the game. It is popular among players because of its versatility; it has stab, [[slash]] and [[crush]]ing options, making it useful on a wide array of monsters, including the [[Corporeal Beast]], to which it deals full damage as a [[Corpbane weapons|Corpbane weapon]]. Most [[dragons]], [[Drake (disambiguation)|drakes]], and [[wyrms]] are also weak to the stab attack type, but as a two-handed weapon it is incompatible with an [[anti-dragon shield]]; it is only advisable to use it in combination with a [[super antifire potion]] (except against wyrms).

Overall, this weapon's total defence bonuses are the highest for any melee weapon, and the second highest for any weapon after the [[Zaryte crossbow]]. Many players consider this weapon to be an upgraded [[dragon spear]] because both of these spears have the same special attack, though the Zamorakian spear cannot be poisoned. Additionally, the Zamorakian spear's stab bonus outweighs its other bonuses, unlike the dragon spear's balanced stats.

[[Otto Godblessed]] can convert the spear into a [[Zamorakian hasta]] for 300,000 [[coins]], or 150,000 coins if the player has completed the [[Elite Kandarin Diary]]. Reversing the hasta into a spear can be done for free by talking to Otto Godblessed again. The hasta has the exact same stats as the spear, but it is one-handed and thus can be used with a [[Shields|shield]] or [[defender]]. This makes the spear inferior to its hasta variant in virtually every situation, except when fighting the Corporeal Beast, as it is weak to spears but not hastae. The hasta can then further be modified to a [[dragon hunter lance]] by combining it with the [[hydra's claw]], faring exceptionally well against [[draconic (attribute|draconic creatures]] and allowing the aforementioned anti-dragon shield to be equipped.

==Special attack==
[[File:Shove (Zamorakian spear).gif|thumb|250px|A player performs the Zamorakian spear's special attack, ''Shove''.]]
The Zamorakian spear has a [[special attacks|special attack]], ''Shove'', which it shares with the [[dragon spear]] and [[Zamorakian hasta]]. It pushes an opponent back and [[Stun (status)|stuns]] them for three seconds, consuming 25% of the player's special attack energy.

The effects of this special are non-stackable, meaning that players cannot use the spear's special attack on a target who is already stunned. Stunned players are also given a one tick (0.6 seconds) period of immunity after a stun wears off in which they cannot be stunned again. In addition to this, as soon as the stun wears off, the stunned player is also granted a 5 tick (3.0 seconds) period of immunity against being bound in place by binding spells like [[Entangle]] or [[Ice Barrage]].

On non-Castle Wars worlds, players within Castle Wars are able to shove targets back-to-back using this special attack.<ref>{{PlainCiteNews|url=https://secure.runescape.com/m=news/poll-83-castle-wars-update?oldschool=1|title=Poll 83: Castle Wars Update|newsdate=2 April 2025}}</ref>

The special attack cannot be used against large monsters, such as [[giants]], because they are too big to push back. If a player tries this, the special attack does not occur and no special attack energy is consumed. A message appears in the chat box, reading, "That creature is too large to knock back!" A general way to tell if the spear can use the "Shove" special attack is to determine how much space the target monster takes up. If it takes up more than one space, it cannot be shoved. For example, a player can shove an [[ice warrior]], which takes up one space, but not an [[ice giant]], which takes up four.
